raw.title: Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 Masonry Lintel & Jamb Design — ASD Reinforced CMU Lintel (REK-06) · Out-of-Plane C&C Wind · Reinforced Jamb (REK-04) · TMS 402-22 TMS 402-22 §5.3 & Ch.8 MDG-2022 REK-04 & REK-06 §8.3.2 Flexure · §8.3.5 Shear §8.3.4.2 Combined Axial+Flexure ASCE 7-22 §2.4 ASD — 0.6W factor Print / Export PDF Save Inputs (.json) Load Inputs (.json) Anderson Rohr Engineering — TMS 402-22 ASD DIAGRAMS — LINTEL ELEVATION & SECTION · JAMB ELEVATION & SECTION 22" min. ext. w = 519 lb/ft w_OOP = 84 lb/ft (0.6×12 psf) P = 17,390 lb a = 5.33 ft Pu = 6,583 lb (uplift) R = 11.5 k R = 11.5 k 10"' clr L = 10.67 ft 8" brg t = 55" d = 52" CMU LINTEL BOT. REINF 1-#6 (22" ext. min.) TOP REINF 1-#4 3-#6 JAMB VERT. (EA. JAMB) Lintel — Elevation b = 7.625" t = 55" d = 52" TOP REINF 1-#4 2-#5 MIN. EA. COURSE #4 SHEAR REINF (TYP. DETAIL) KNOCK-OUT WEB BOND BEAM BOTTOM REINF 1-#6 Lintel — Section w_OOP = 24 lb/ft (0.6W) P_oop = 420 lb 3-#6 VERT. IN GROUTED CELLS P = R = 11,468 lb H = 14.0 ft H_s = 10.0 ft W = 24" CMU JAMB Jamb — Elevation w_OOP (0.6W) 3-#6 VERT. W = 24" b = 7.625" d = 3.8" Jamb — Section ■ 1. Material Properties CMU Nominal Size 6″ CMU — b = 5.625″ 8″ CMU — b = 7.625″ 12″ CMU — b = 11.625″ b = 7.625″ f′m (psi) 1,500 1,750 2,000 2,500 3,000 Steel Grade Grade 40 — Fs = 20,000 psi Grade 60 — Fs = 32,000 psi Mortar Type (for Ft) Type S Type N Affects OOP flexural tension allowable Ft ■ 2. Opening & Lintel Geometry TMS 402-22 §5.3.1 Clear Opening Span (ft) Bearing Length Each End (in) Min 4″ per §5.3.1.4; 8″ typical Total Lintel Depth t (in) Multiples of 8″ (CMU courses) Effective Depth d (in) Auto = t − 3.5″; adjust if needed Wall Thickness b (in, auto) ■ 3. Wall & Jamb Geometry REK-04 Total Wall Height Hwall (ft) Full wall height; used for wind tributary to lintel Lintel Soffit Height Hsoffit (ft) Floor to bottom of lintel Jamb Width Wjamb (in) Width of masonry pier at each side of opening Jamb Height Hjamb (ft) Full wall height (jamb spans floor to top of wall) Jamb Steel Cover (in) Cover to bar center from OOP wall face. Default 3.81″ = b/2 → centered bars (typical). Reduce only if bars are detailed offset toward the tension face. ■ 4. Lintel Gravity Loads ASD Service Combo D+L Enter floor/roof tributary load only in wtrib. CMU wall self-weight above the opening is computed automatically from the unit weight and geometry (Hwall − Hsoffit). Total lintel load shown below is used in all calculations. wtrib — Floor/Roof Tributary (lb/ft) Floor or roof tributary only — do NOT include wall weight here CMU Unit Weight wwall,psf (psf) Wall weight/ft height incl. grout. Auto-added: wwall = wwall,psf × (Hwall−Hsoffit) wwall — Auto Wall Weight (lb/ft, auto) = wwall,psf × (Hwall − Hsoffit) ft wtotal — Total Lintel Load (lb/ft, auto) = wtrib + wwall — used in all calculations Pconc — Concentrated Load (lb) 0 if none P Location from Left (ft) Auto = midspan wuplift — Uplift Combo (lb/ft) 0.6D combination if net uplift; 0 if N/A Puplift — Uplift Load (lb) ■ 5. Wind Loading — Out-of-Plane C&C Nominal Pressure Input Wind load level: Enter the nominal ASCE 7 strength-level C&C design pressure from the C&C chart (unfactored). The calculator applies the 0.6W ASD factor (ASCE 7-22 §2.4.1, combo 6: D + 0.6W) internally to all wind demands. Note: TMS 402 eliminated the one-third allowable stress increase in the 2011 edition (allowable values are already calibrated for wind/seismic) — leave the checkbox below unchecked unless your jurisdiction's alternative basic load combinations specifically permit it. Nominal C&C Pressure W (psf, strength level) ASCE 7 unfactored design pressure — calc applies 0.6× for ASD ×0.6 → ASD Wind 0.6W (psf, auto) = 0.6 × nominal; used for all demand calculations ×H/2 → Lintel OOP Line Load (lb/ft, auto) = 0.6W × Hwall/2 (trib. to lintel) Apply ⅓ allowable stress increase (multiplies wind-load allowables by 4/3). Not permitted by TMS 402-22 with ASCE 7 §2.4 combinations — the one-third increase was eliminated in 2011. Only check if using IBC alternative basic load combinations that permit it. ■ 6. Lintel Reinforcement Bottom Bars (qty) 1 2 3 Bottom Bar Size #4 — 0.20 in² #5 — 0.31 in² #6 — 0.44 in² #7 — 0.60 in² #8 — 0.79 in² #9 — 1.00 in² Top Bars (qty, uplift) 1 2 3 Top Bar Size #4 — 0.20 in² #5 — 0.31 in² #6 — 0.44 in² #7 — 0.60 in² #8 — 0.79 in² #9 — 1.00 in² Lintel OOP Cover (in) Cover to bar center from OOP face (b direction) — for reinforced OOP tension check when bars are present Shear Reinforcement Bar Size #3 — 0.11 in² #4 — 0.20 in² #5 — 0.31 in² Vertical shear tie/stirrup Av (used only if shear reinforcement is provided) Shear Reinforcement Spacing s (in) Tie spacing per S802 schedule; §8.3.5.2.1 limit = min(d/2, 48″) Provide shear reinforcement in lintel (adds Fvs per Eq. 8-24). Leave unchecked to check the masonry-only shear capacity Fvm (Eq. 8-23); if fv exceeds Fvm the calc will flag that shear reinforcement is required per §8.3.5.2. ■ 7. Jamb Vertical Reinforcement REK-04 Vertical bars resist OOP wind bending. Bars assumed at d = bwall − cover from the tension wall face. Number of Vertical Bars 1 2 3 4 5 6 Typically 1 per 8″ CMU cell across jamb width Jamb Bar Size #4 — 0.20 in² #5 — 0.31 in² #6 — 0.44 in² #7 — 0.60 in² #8 — 0.79 in² #9 — 1.00 in² Bearing Eccentricity e (in) Eccentricity of lintel reaction from jamb centroid (OOP direction). Creates additional OOP bending in jamb. Use 0 if centered or unknown. ■ 8. Jamb Demands REK-04 M/V/P Demand source: By default the jamb checks derive M, V, and P internally from the lintel reactions and wall geometry above (unchanged behavior). Select Direct entry to drive the same jamb check machinery — cracked-section P+M solve (§8.3.4.2.2), slender ¼Pe cap (Eq. 8-10/8-14), axial (Eq. 8-16), and shear (§8.3.5.1) — with externally computed demands, e.g. the M/V/P interaction points printed in MDG Example REK-04 ASD. Enter ASD-combination (service-level) values: the 0.6 wind factor must already be included in the entered demands (it is NOT applied again). Jamb Demand Source Derived from lintel reactions (default) Direct entry — M / V / P Direct mode bypasses the internal wind/reaction jamb demand model; bearing checks still use the lintel reactions M — Jamb OOP Moment (lb·in, ASD) e.g. REK-04: 39,775 or 45,685 lb·in V — Jamb OOP Shear (lb, ASD) e.g. REK-04: 773 or 964 lb P — Jamb Axial (lb, ASD, compression +) e.g. REK-04: 15,026 or 4,639 lb ▶ Run All Checks ✓ ALL CHECKS PASS Max D/C = 0.938 · f′m = 1750.0 psi · Lclr = 10.0 ft · tlin = 55.0″ · Wjamb = 24.0″ · Wnom = 20.0 psf → 0.6W = 12.0 psf ▮ PART A — Lintel Strong Direction (Gravity) · REK-06 ASD ✓ PASS — Lintel Gravity — REK-06 · Max D/C = 0.938 ⇄ ResultsJamb axial (gov. lintel rxn + self-wt): 11.47 kips→Lintel left reaction: 11.47 kips→Lintel right reaction: 11.46 kips→Lintel gravity moment: 53.75 kip-ft→Lintel gravity shear: 11.47 kips→Total lintel line load: 519 lb/ft 519.0 lb/ft Trib Load w_trib (floor/roof only) 0 lb/ft Wall Weight w_wall auto (0.0 psf×4.0 ft) 519.0 lb/ft Total w_total (used in all calcs) 53,755 lb·ft Max Gravity Moment M 11,468 lb Max Shear V (at support) 30021.8 psi Steel Stress f_s vs Fs=32000.0 364.6 psi Masonry Stress f_b vs Fb=787.5 11,468 lb Left Reaction R_L → jamb axial 11,458 lb Right Reaction R_R → jamb axial Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Bottom Steel fs As = 0.44 in² ; n = 18.4 ; j = 0.939 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs = 30021.8 psi Fs = 32000.0 psi 0.938 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 — Flexure ASD (Bottom Bars) Load: w trib = 519.0 + w wall = 0 = w total = 519.0 lb/ft [w wall = 0.0 psf×4.0 ft] M = 53,755 lb·ft (combined uniform + concentrated) REK-06 printed benchmark: M = 53,771 lb·ft; printed conversion = 645,251 lb·in . The live value above is computed from the entered loads and retained separately. nρ = 18.41×0.0011 = 0.0204 k = √ (nρ)²+2nρ − nρ = 0.183 j = 1−k/3 = 0.939 fs = M As⋅j⋅d = 645,056 0.44×0.939×52.0 = 30021.8 psi D/C = 30021.8 32000.0 = 0.938 Masonry Compression fb k = 0.183 ; j = 0.939 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2.2.1 fb = 364.6 psi Fb = 787.5 psi 0.463 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2.2.1 — Masonry Compressive Stress Fb = 0.45 f′ m = 787.5 psi fb = 2M b⋅kd⋅jd = 2×645,056 7.6×9.5×48.8 = 364.6 psi D/C = 364.6 787.5 = 0.463 Shear fv V = 11,468 lb ; M/Vdv = 1.00 TMS 402-22 §8.3.5 fv = 28.9 psi Fv = 47.1 psi 0.615 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.5 — Shear (Masonry Only) fv = V Anv = 11,468 7.6×52.0 = 28.9 psi [Eq. 8-19; A nv =b⋅d v , d v =52.0″] Fvm = ½[4−1.75 M/Vd v ]√ f′m + 0.25 P/A n = 0.5(4−1.75×1.00)×√ 1750.0 + 0 = 47.1 psi [Eq. 8-23; P=0 on lintel] Fv = (F vm +F vs )γ g ≤ 2.00γ g √ f′m [Eq. 8-20/8-21/8-22, γ g =1.0 grouted] = 47.1 psi (cap 83.7 psi) D/C = 28.9 47.1 = 0.615 Top Steel (Uplift) Uplift: 0.6D combo TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs = 12258.7 psi Fs = 32000.0 psi 0.383 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 — Flexure ASD (Top Bars, Uplift) |Mup| = 10,173 lb·ft fs,top = 12258.7 psi ; f b,top = 97.1 psi Governing D/C = 0.383 Min. Bearing Length 8″ typical TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.4 bearing = 8.0 in. ≥ 4.0 in. 0.500 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.4 — Minimum Bearing Length Required: bearing ≥ 4 in. at each end Provided: bearing = 8.0 in. ✓ D/C = 4.0 8.0 = 0.500 Deflection Gate Waived: L ≤ 8d TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.6 L/8d = 0.31 ≤ 1.0 (waived) 0.308 Info ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.6 — Deflection Check Gate §5.3.1.6.1: deflection need not be checked when L ≤ 8d. L / 8d = 128.0″ 8×52.0″ = 0.31 L/8d = 0.31 ≤ 1.0 — deflection check waived per §5.3.1.6.1 (immediate deflection ≈ L/600). Lateral Bracing (Advisory) Continuously braced by wall (typical) TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.3 Lb,max = 134.2 in. — — Info ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.3 — Compression-Face Lateral Bracing (Advisory) Max unbraced length: min(32b, 120b²/d) = min(32×7.6, 120×7.6²/52.0) = min(244.0, 134.2) = 134.2 in. In typical construction the lintel compression face is continuously braced by the masonry wall above — this limit is normally satisfied by inspection. ρ vs ρ_bal (Advisory) Steel-controlled (ductile) TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2.2 ρ/ρbal = 0.01 — — Info ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2.2 (Commentary) — Reinforcement Ratio (Advisory) ρ = A s /(b⋅d) = 0.0011 ρ bal = 0.1559 ρ/ρ bal = 0.01 ρ/ρ bal < ~0.38 — steel-controlled (tension-governed), ductile behavior. ▮ PART B — Lintel Out-of-Plane Wind · 0.6W C&C ✓ PASS — Lintel Out-of-Plane Wind (0.6W ASD) · Max D/C = 0.497 20.0 psf Nominal C&C W (input, unfactored) 12.0 psf ASD Wind 0.6W (used in calcs) 84.0 lb/ft OOP Line Load 0.6W×H/2 1,195 lb·ft OOP Moment M_oop (weak axis) 26.9 psi OOP Stress vs Fb=583.3 448 lb OOP Shear to Each Jamb 0.497 Biaxial Interaction D/C Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail OOP Compressive Stress fb,oop 0.6W = 12.0 psf ; Soop = 533.0 in³ TMS 402-22 §8.2.3 fb,oop = 26.9 psi Fb,oop = 583.3 psi 0.046 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.3 — Lintel OOP Compressive Stress (Unreinforced, Weak Axis) Input (nominal): W = 20.0 psf ✖ 0.6 (ASCE 7-22 §2.4.1 combo 6) = 12.0 psf ASD Woop = 0.6W × H wall /2 = 12.0×14.0/2 = 84.0 lb/ft Moop = Woop×L² 8 = 84.0×10.67² 8 ×12 = 14,336 lb·in [L = effective span] Soop = tlin×b² 6 = 55.0×7.6² 6 = 533.0 in³ (weak axis, bending about vertical) fb,oop = Moop Soop = 14,336 533.0 = 26.9 psi Fb,oop = f′ m /3 (Eq. 8-13) = 583.3 = 583.3 psi D/C = 26.9 583.3 = 0.046 OOP Tension — Reinforced fs,oop Bars present; doop=5.1″ ; k=0.213 ; j=0.929 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs,oop = 6842.6 psi Fs = 32000.0 psi 0.214 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 — Lintel OOP Tension (Reinforced — Bottom Bars Present) Reinforced check used: A s,bot = 0.44 in² resists OOP tension. Unreinforced F t limit not applicable when reinforcement is present. doop = b − cover oop = 7.6−2.5 = 5.1 in (OOP effective depth) nρoop = 18.41×0.0016 = 0.0287 k oop = 0.213 j oop = 0.929 fs,oop = Moop As⋅joop⋅doop = 14,336 0.44×0.929×5.1 = 6842.6 psi Fs,eff = 32000.0 = 32000.0 psi D/C = 6842.6 32000.0 = 0.214 Biaxial Bending Interaction Gravity + OOP at compression corner TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fb,grav Fb + fb,oop Fb,oop ≤ 1.0 0.497 PASS ▶ Calc Biaxial Bending Interaction — Compression Corner Interaction = fb,grav Fb + fb,oop Fb,oop ≤ 1.0 = 364.6 787.5 + 26.9 787.5 = 0.463 + 0.034 = 0.497 D/C = 0.497 ▮ PART C — Masonry Jamb (Reinforced) · REK-04 ASD ✓ PASS — Masonry Jamb REK-04 · Max D/C = 0.333 11,468 lb Jamb Axial P (lintel rxn + self-wt) 62.7 psi Axial Stress f_a vs Fa=369.0 24.0 lb/ft C&C on Jamb 0.6W×Wj/12 448 lb OOP Pt Load from Lintel 1,033 ft·lb Max Jamb Wind Moment M_j 0.0 psi Jamb Steel f_s (P+M) vs Fs=32000.0 0.147 Combined P+M D/C (cracked sec.) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Jamb Width ≥ Bearing Geometry: pier ≥ bearing dim. TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.4 Bearing = 8.0 in. Wjamb = 24.0 in. 0.333 PASS ▶ Calc Bearing Geometry — Jamb Width vs Bearing Length (see §5.3.1.4) Basis: §5.3.1.4 sets the minimum bearing LENGTH (≥4″, checked in Part A). This row confirms the jamb pier is wide enough to seat that bearing. Required: W jamb ≥ bearing = 8.0 in. Provided: W jamb = 24.0 in. D/C = 8.0 24.0 = 0.333 Bearing Stress fbrg Abrg = 61.0 in² TMS 402-22 §8.1.6 fbrg = 188.0 psi Fbr = 577.5 psi 0.326 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.6 — Allowable Bearing Stress Fbr = 0.33 f′ m = 577.5 psi Abrg = 8.0″×7.6″ = 61.0 in² fbrg = Rlintel Abrg = 11,468 61.0 = 188.0 psi D/C = 188.0 577.5 = 0.326 Axial Stress fa h/r = 76.3 ; r = 2.20 in. TMS 402-22 §8.3.4 fa = 62.7 psi Fa = 369.0 psi 0.170 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2.1, Eq 8-16/8-17 — Allowable Axial Stress (with Slenderness) Fa = 0.30f′ m [1−(h/140r)²] (Eq 8-16; 0.65A st F s term neglected) h/r = 76.3 ≤ 99 r = b/√12 = 2.20 in. F a,base = 369.0 psi P = R lintel + P sw = 11,468 + 0 = 11,468 lb [P sw = 0.0 psf×14.0 ft×2.00 ft] fa = P Ag = 11,468 24.0×7.6 = 62.7 psi D/C = 62.7 369.0 = 0.170 Jamb OOP Steel fs,j Mj = 1,033 ft·lb ; 0.6W applied TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs,j = 2965.6 psi Fs = 32000.0 psi 0.093 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 — Jamb OOP Steel Stress (REK-04) Wind (0.6W applied): 1. C&C on jamb: q = 0.6W×W j /12 = 12.0×24.0/12 = 24.0 lb/ft 2. Lintel OOP reaction: P oop = 448 lb at h = 12.29 ft Rbot = 222.7 lb Mmax,j = 1,033 ft·lb = 12,395 in·lb nρ = 18.41×0.0144 = 0.2655 ; k j = 0.510 ; j j = 0.830 fs,j = Mj As⋅j⋅d = 12,395 1.32×0.830×3.81 = 2965.6 psi Fs,eff = 32000.0 = 32000.0 psi D/C = 2965.6 32000.0 = 0.093 Jamb OOP Masonry fb,j bj = 24.0 in ; d = 3.81 in TMS 402-22 §8.3.2.2.1 fb,j = 167.7 psi Fb = 787.5 psi 0.213 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2.2.1 — Jamb OOP Masonry Stress Fb = 0.45f′ m = 787.5 psi fb,j = 2Mj bj⋅kd⋅jd = 2×12,395 24.0×1.9×3.2 = 167.7 psi D/C = 167.7 787.5 = 0.213 Slender Stability Cap P≤¼Pe h/r = 76.3 ; e = 0.00″ TMS 402-22 Eq. 8-10 P = 11,468 lb ¼Pe = 122,082 lb 0.094 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.4.1, Eq. 8-10 / Eq. 8-14 — Slender Stability Cap (P ≤ ¼Pe) Eq. 8-14: P e = π²EmIn h² (1−0.577 e/r)³ E m = 900f′ m = 1,575,000 psi ; I n = W j b³/12 = 887 in⁴ ; h = 14.0×12 = 168.0″ (pin-pin) e = 0.00″ ; r = 2.20″ ; (1−0.577×0.00)³ = 1.000 Pe = 488,328 lb ¼P e = 122,082 lb P = 11,468 lb (lintel rxn + self-wt) D/C = 11,468 122,082 = 0.094 Combined P+M (Cracked Section) Uncracked ; c=7.63″ TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2.2 fb=116.0 / fs=0.0 psi 0.45f′m / Fs 0.147 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2.1/§8.3.4.2.2 — Combined Axial + Flexure (Cracked-Section P+M Solve) Method: linear-elastic transformed section (W j ×b, steel at d=3.81″, n=18.41), solved for neutral-axis depth c from P+M equilibrium (no superposition). Demand: P = 11,468 lb (comp) ; M = M wind = 12,395 lb·in Section state: UNCRACKED ; kd = c = 7.63″ (full depth — no tension edge) fb = 116.0 psi ≤ 0.45f′ m = 787.5 psi (D/C = 0.147) fs = 0.0 psi ≤ F s = 32000.0 psi (D/C = 0.000) Governing D/C = 0.147 (max of masonry 0.147 , steel 0.000) Jamb Average Shear Derived support V TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.1 V = 223 lb; fv=2.43 psi Fv=62.73 psi 0.039 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.1 — Jamb Average Shear V = 223 lb (derived support shear); A nv = W j d = 24.0×3.81 = 91.6 in² f v = V/A nv = 2.43 psi ; M/(Vd) = 1.000 F vm = ½(4−1.75M/Vd)√f′ m + 0.25P/A n = 62.73 psi ; upper bound = 83.67 psi; F v = 62.73 psi D/C = 2.43 62.73 = 0.039 Engineering Notes: Design per TMS 402-22 ASD / MDG-2022 REK-04 (jamb) and REK-06 (lintel). Enter gravity loads per ASCE 7-22 §2.4 ASD combinations. Wind convention: Enter the nominal ASCE 7 strength-level C&C pressure from the design chart. The calculator multiplies by 0.6 (ASCE 7-22 §2.4.1, combo D + 0.6W) for all wind demands. TMS 402 eliminated the one-third allowable stress increase in 2011 — allowables (including Table 8.2.4.2 flexural tension) are already calibrated for wind/seismic, so no further increase should be applied with §2.4 combinations. Effective lintel span: L = clear + bearing/12 per §5.3.1.1. Minimum bearing 4″ is enforced as a check (Part A) per §5.3.1.4; 8″ typical. Both the lintel gravity moment/shear AND the lintel out-of-plane wind moment are computed over this effective span L. Lintel shear (§8.3.5): fv = V/Anv (Eq. 8-19). Masonry allowable Fvm per Eq. 8-23 (including the +0.25 P/An term; P = 0 on the lintel). When "provide shear reinforcement" is enabled, Fvs = 0.5⋅AvFsdv/(Anvs) is added (Eq. 8-24) and Fv = (Fvm+Fvs)γg capped by γg(2–3)√f′m (Eq. 8-20/8-21/8-22). Lintel modeled fully grouted → γg = 1.0. If fv exceeds Fvm with no reinforcement provided, the calc flags "shear reinforcement required per §8.3.5.2" (max spacing min(d/2, 48″), §8.3.5.2.1). Shear is taken at the support (conservative; §8.3.5 permits d/2). Deflection (§5.3.1.6): deflection need not be checked when L ≤ 8d (§5.3.1.6.1; immediate deflection ≈ L/600). When L > 8d the calc emits a WARNING that a manual Ieff evaluation is required per §5.3.1.6.2 (Eq. 5-1), with the cracking moment Mcr computed from the Table 8.2.4.2 allowable flexural tensile stress × 2.5 and the deflection limited to L/600 (§4.6). Integral-wall end restraint (20–45% reduction, commentary) is not automatically credited. Lintel OOP line load: Woop = 0.6W × Hwall/2. OOP section modulus Soop = tlin×b²/6 (bending about vertical axis). When lintel bars are present, OOP tension is checked as a reinforced section (steel stress check, doop = b − OOP cover). When no bars are present, the unreinforced flexural tensile limit Ft from Table 8.2.4.2 governs. Jamb OOP model: simply supported vertical beam with (1) uniform C&C = 0.6W×Wj/12 distributed load, and (2) lintel OOP reaction as point load at mid-lintel height. Zero-shear method locates max moment. Jamb axial demand = governing lintel reaction + jamb tributary self-weight Psw = wwall,psf × Hjamb × (Wjamb/12) to the base section. Bearing stress (§8.1.6) uses only the lintel reaction (self-weight acts below the bearing plane). Allowable axial Fa per Eq. 8-16/8-17 (0.30 f′m; the 0.65 AstFs steel term is conservatively neglected). Jamb slender-column stability (Eq. 8-10): P ≤ ¼Pe, with Pe = π²EmIn/h² × (1 − 0.577 e/r)³ (Eq. 8-14), Em = 900 f′m, In = Wjambb³/12, h = Hjamb×12 (pin-pin), e = bearing eccentricity. The cubic term is clamped ≥ 0 and the calc flags when e/r exceeds the Eq. 8-11 validity range (e > r/0.577). Jamb combined axial + flexure (§8.3.4.2.2): solved by a linear-elastic cracked-section P+M analysis (transformed section Wjamb×b, single steel layer at d, modular ratio n) — the neutral-axis depth c is found from P+M equilibrium (no stress superposition). fb ≤ 0.45 f′m and fs ≤ Fs. When P+M leaves no net tension the gross (uncracked) section is used. No moment magnifier is applied — TMS ASD credits the axial-load safety factor (§8.3.3 commentary) and limits stability via Eq. 8-10. The unreinforced interaction Eq. 8-9 / "Eq. 8-20" is NOT used (per commentary it does not apply to reinforced masonry; Eq. 8-20 is the shear equation). Bearing eccentricity e (in): the lintel reaction acts eccentric from the jamb centroid (OOP direction), adding Mecc = Rlintel × e. This is combined with the OOP wind moment in the cracked-section P+M solve above. Enter 0 if the load is centered. Jamb effective depth d = bwall − cover. Default cover 3.81″ = b/2 assumes centered-cell bars (d = b/2, typical). If d exceeds b/2 the calc flags that offset bars are assumed — verify the placement is detailed and enforceable. No 1/3 stress increase applied (consistent with TMS 402-22, which eliminated the increase in 2011). Wall self-weight is automatically computed from wwall,psf × (Hwall − Hsoffit) and added to the floor/roof tributary load. Enter only the floor/roof tributary load in wtrib. Total wtotal = wtrib + wwall is used for all moment, shear, and reaction calculations. Print button generates a clean printout/PDF of all inputs and results. Save/Load buttons store and retrieve input sets as .json files for different opening configurations. ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub× Total lintel line load519 lb/ft Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel gravity shear11.47 kips Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel gravity moment53.75 kip-ft Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel right reaction11.46 kips Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel left reaction11.47 kips Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Jamb axial (gov. lintel rxn + self-wt)11.47 kips Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Max D/C ratio0.938 Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel line load519 lb/ft Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 ⤓ Use here (w_load)Copy Right reaction11.46 kips Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Left reaction11.47 kips Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel max shear11.47 kips Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel max moment53.75 kip-ft Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Max D/C ratio1.037 Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Required steel area0.415 in^2 Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Allowable shear stress47.06 psi Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Design shear0.964 kips Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Design moment3.807 kip-ft Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Anchor T+V Interaction0 Masonry Top-of-Wall Anchor — ASD | TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3 · 7/11/2026 Copy Anchor Allow.
